# FORMAT: one row per (test-name
# once per listed engine version
column2: so a test fans out across versions by adding rows.

column1: # test-name MUST be a directory under e2e-tests/tests/. Lines starting with '#' and
# blank lines are ignored. (docs/architecture/11-testing-qa.md §3.2)
# This is the smoke suite: every case on the charter-default engine only.
# Keep it small and fast — it is the per-PR signal
# ENGINE VERSION: pinned to 9.1.0 — the only published percona/valkey GA tag. The CR
# templates substitute it into `image: percona/valkey:${VALKEY_VERSION}`
# MUST exist on the registry. The older 9.0/8.0/7.2 lines are collapsed to 9.1.0 until
# percona/valkey publishes those engine tags; re-add multi-version rows then (and
# restore the migration-gate floor in hack/lint-csv.sh).
# WHY a CSV and not a hard-coded list: the engine-version fan-out and the per-suite
# selection (pr / distro / minikube / release) are DATA
# by hack/lint-csv.sh. A typo in the version column silently SKIPS a row in the older
# bash harnesses; hack/lint-csv.sh turns that silent skip into a hard failure (missing
# test dir / bad version / migration-or-scale test on an unsupported engine).
# test-name
